name: AUP-V5-LLM-cache-Berlin-Ortsteile
description: Bundled prompt-response cache from the V5 (LLM-decide) decision engine on the 96-zone Berlin Ortsteile benchmark instance of the AUP framework. The cache contains 80,124 prompt-response pairs (40,062 baseline + 40,062 shock) generated under temperature 0 against an API-pinned foundation-model snapshot. It enables Tier 4 bit-identical reproduction of the V5 headline numbers in the AUP paper without consuming LLM credits. Each cache entry is a JSON file keyed by <persona-cluster-id>_<iteration>_<stage>.json under the baseline/ and shock/ subdirectories.
citeAs: AUP-Berlin Benchmark V5 LLM Cache, version 0.1.0, 2026.
rai:dataCollection: Generated via deterministic prompts (full prompt templates in Appendix C of the AUP paper) against an API-pinned foundation-model snapshot at sampling temperature 0. No human subjects were involved and no third-party data was ingested. Inputs to each prompt are derived from the public Berlin Ortsteile aggregation and the East-West Express tau-shock specification, both of which are bundled with the AUP codebase.
rai:annotationCollection: No human annotation. Each cache entry stores the raw structured LLM response, parsed into (residence_zone, workplace_zone) action assignments and a 0-100 self-rated satisfaction score. The parser implementation is in src/simulator/decisions/llm.py of the AUP codebase.
rai:dataUseCases: (i) Tier 4 bit-identical reproduction of the AUP-Berlin V5 headline numbers without LLM credits. (ii) Pedagogical illustration of LLM-driven choice-rule behaviour under spatial-equilibrium clearing for graduate coursework. (iii) Comparative analysis with alternative decision engines plugged into the AUP DecisionEngine interface.
rai:dataLimitations: Cache entries inherit any biases of the foundation-model snapshot at the time of generation (training-data priors plausibly biased toward Western-metropolis defaults; see Appendix B of the AUP paper). The cache is bit-identical to the underlying snapshot and does not generalise to other foundation models or sampling temperatures.

distribution.name: llm_cache_v5.tar.gz
distribution.description: Compressed tarball of the V5 LLM cache. Decompresses to roughly 320 MB containing two top-level directories baseline/ and shock/, each holding tens of thousands of JSON files.

recordSet.name: cache-entries
recordSet.description: Per-iteration LLM prompt-response records keyed by persona cluster, market iteration, and decision stage.

field.name: persona_cluster_id
field.description: Index of the K=50 demographic cluster (0-49).

field.name: prompt
field.description: Full prompt sent to the foundation model.

field.name: satisfaction_score
field.description: LLM-elicited 0-100 self-rated satisfaction score, used as V5's per-agent welfare contribution.
